rationale: The citing paper’s prior-work statement explicitly says it moves beyond static choice-set advertising treatments in “Draganska and Klapper (2011),” which matches this cited DOI (10.1509/jmkr.48.4.653). The cited paper’s core contribution—separating advertising’s awareness/choice-set effect from utility effects in aggregate discrete-choice demand—directly overlaps with the citing paper’s central mechanism, so this is not an obvious incidental mention.
